_comment: Reusable code templates shown in the trainer's Templates tab. Each: a memorize-able phrase, the one question to ask before writing the loop, and a skeleton. `code` may be a string or an array of lines (joined with newlines). Add your own here, or jot them in-app (those live in your browser and are included in Export).

templates.title: Backtracking (DFS)
templates.when: subsets · permutations · combinations · word search · N-queens · partitions
templates.question: What choices are available at this exact level?

templates.title: Concurrent level-order BFS (thread-pool fan-out)
templates.when: web crawler · parallel BFS over slow I/O (network / disk)
templates.phrase: Fan out the fetch. Fold in on one thread.
templates.question: What's the slow call to parallelize, and what shared state must stay single-threaded?
code: # fan out the SLOW call across the whole level...
# ...but merge on THIS thread only -> no lock needed
if keep(n) and n not in visited:
# web crawler: get_neighbors = htmlParser.getUrls,  keep = same-host filter
